1586	
1587	/// Parser callback invoked inside `pcall`: reads the first byte to decide
1588	/// binary vs. text, then calls the undumper or parser accordingly.
1589	///
1590	/// C: `static void f_parser(lua_State *L, void *ud)`
1591	fn f_parser(state: &mut LuaState, p: &mut SParser) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1592	    // C: int c = zgetc(p->z)  — read first character
1593	    // zgetc → z.getc()  (macros.tsv)
1594	    let c = p.z.getc();
1595	
1596	    // C: if (c == LUA_SIGNATURE[0])
1597	    // LUA_SIGNATURE → const LUA_SIGNATURE: &[u8] = b"\x1bLua"  (macros.tsv)
1598	    let cl = if c == b'\x1b' as i32 {
1599	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "binary")
1600	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"binary")?;
1601	        // C: cl = luaU_undump(L, p->z, p->name)
1602	        // TODO(port): undump returns a LClosure; the Rust API isn't finalised.
1603	        crate::undump::undump(state, &mut p.z, &p.name)?
1604	    } else {
1605	        // C: checkmode(L, p->mode, "text")
1606	        check_mode(state, p.mode.as_deref(), b"text")?;
1607	        // C: cl = luaY_parser(L, p->z, &p->buff, &p->dyd, p->name, c)
1608	        // TODO(port): parser API not yet finalised; returns a LClosure.
1609	        parse_stub(state, &mut p.z, &mut p.buff, &mut p.dyd, &p.name, c)?
1610	    };
1611	
1612	    // C: lua_assert(cl->nupvalues == cl->p->sizeupvalues)
1613	    debug_assert!(cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len());
1614	    // C: luaF_initupvals(L, cl)
1615	    func::init_upvals(state, &cl)?;
1616	
1617	    // PORT NOTE: In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` / `luaU_undump` themselves push the
1618	    // closure onto the stack before returning (see lparser.c `luaY_parser`:
1619	    // `setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L);`). In the Rust port
1620	    // they return the closure by value, so `f_parser` must push it here.
1621	    // Without this, the caller (`api::load`) sees stale Nil at top-1 and any
1622	    // subsequent `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` fails with "attempt to call a nil
1623	    // value".
1624	    state.check_stack(1)?;
1625	    state.push(LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)));
1626	
1627	    Ok(())
1628	}
1629	
1630	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1631	///
1632	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1633	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1634	    state: &mut LuaState,
1635	    z: ZIO,
1636	    name: &[u8],
1637	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1638	) -> LuaStatus {
1639	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1640	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1641	    state.inc_nny();
1642	
1643	    let mut p = SParser {
1644	        z,
1645	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1646	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1647	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1648	        name: name.to_vec(),
1649	    };
1650	
1651	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1652	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1653	
1654	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1655	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1656	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1657	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1658	
1659	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)

2760	/// PORT NOTE: In C, returns `LClosure *` (a GC object). In Rust (Phase A),
2761	///   we return `Box<LuaProto>` since we don't have GcRef<LuaLClosure> ready.
2762	///   Phase B will wrap this in a proper LuaLClosure / GcRef.
2763	pub fn parse(
2764	    state: &mut LuaState,
2765	    dyd: DynData,
2766	    source: &[u8],
2767	    name: &[u8],
2768	    firstchar: i32,
2769	) -> Result<Box<LuaProto>, LuaError> {
2770	    let source_str = state.intern_str(name)?;
2771	    let envn_str = state.intern_str(lua_lex::LUA_ENV)?;
2772	
2773	    let rest_bytes: Vec<u8> = source.iter().skip(1).copied().collect();
2774	    let z = lua_lex::ZIO::from_bytes(rest_bytes);
2775	
2776	    let lex_ls = lua_lex::LexState {
2777	        current: firstchar,
2778	        linenumber: 1,
2779	        lastline: 1,
2780	        t: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
2781	        lookahead: lua_lex::Token::eos(),
2782	        fs: None,
2783	        z,
2784	        buff: lua_lex::LexBuffer::new(),
2785	        h: None,
2786	        dyd: None,
2787	        source: source_str.0.clone(),
2788	        envn: envn_str.0.clone(),
2789	    };
2790	
2791	    let mut lexstate = LexState {
2792	        current: lex_ls.current,
2793	        linenumber: lex_ls.linenumber,
2794	        lastline: lex_ls.lastline,
2795	        t: LexToken::default(),
2796	        lookahead: LexToken::default(),
2797	        fs: None,
2798	        dyd,
2799	        source: Some(source_str.clone()),
2800	        envn: Some(GcRef(lex_ls.envn.clone())),
2801	        lex: lex_ls,
2802	    };
2803	    // C: luaX_setinput is the only setup the C parser performs before
2804	    //   `mainfunc`; it does NOT pre-read the first token. `mainfunc` itself
2805	    //   issues the initial `luaX_next` once its prelude (open_func, vararg
2806	    //   marker, _ENV upvalue) is in place.
2807	
2808	    let mut main_proto = Box::new(LuaProto::placeholder());
2809	    main_proto.source = Some(source_str);
2810	    main_proto.is_vararg = true;
2811	    let main_fs = FuncState {
2812	        f: main_proto,
2813	        prev: None,
2814	        bl: None,
2815	        pc: 0,
2816	        lasttarget: 0,
2817	        previousline: 0,
2818	        nk: 0,
2819	        np: 0,
2820	        nabslineinfo: 0,
2821	        firstlocal: 0,
2822	        firstlabel: 0,
2823	        ndebugvars: 0,
2824	        nactvar: 0,
2825	        nups: 0,
2826	        freereg: 0,
2827	        iwthabs: 0,
2828	        needclose: false,
2829	    };
2830	
2831	    mainfunc(&mut lexstate, state, main_fs)
2832	}
2833	
2834	/// Convert a `lua_lex::TokenValue` into the local `parse::TokenValue` flat shape.
2835	///
2836	/// The parser's local `LexState` predates the lex-side enum and uses a flat
2837	/// (r, i, ts) record; this picks out whichever variant the lexer produced.
2838	fn local_token_value(v: &lua_lex::TokenValue) -> TokenValue {
2839	    match v {
2840	        lua_lex::TokenValue::None => TokenValue::default(),
2841	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Float(r) => TokenValue { r: *r, i: 0, ts: None },
2842	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Int(i) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: *i, ts: None },
2843	        lua_lex::TokenValue::Str(s) => TokenValue { r: 0.0, i: 0, ts: Some(GcRef(s.clone())) },
2844	    }
2845	}
2846

delta.text: Now I understand the b
ug. The C-Lua `luaY_parser` allocates the LClosure with the right number of upval slots up-front (always 1 for the main chunk's
`_ENV`). The Rust `parse()` returns just a `Box<LuaProto>`, and the `parser_hook` in the CLI constructs the closure with `upvals: Vec::new
()` — but `proto.upvalues.len()` is 1 (the `_ENV` upvalue added by `mainf
unc`). Fix: construct the closure with upvals matching `proto.upvalues.len()`.

content.text: Now I understand the bug. The C-Lua `luaY_parser` allocates the LClosure with the right number of upval slots up-front (always 1 for the main chunk's `_ENV`). The Rust `parse()` returns just a `Box<LuaProto>`, and the `parser_hook` in the CLI constructs the closure with `upvals: Vec::new()` — but `proto.upvalues.len()` is 1 (the `_ENV` upvalue added by `mainfunc`). Fix: construct the closure with upvals matching `proto.upvalues.len()`.

delta.text: The
fix worked — the original assertion at `do_.rs:1613
` is gone. Step 3/4 now passes and step 4/4 (Executing chunk) hits a new error at `state.rs:1382` (
index out of bounds, len 0 / index 0). That's a different bug — the next blocker for

the loop.

## Report

**Root cause:** `parser_hook` in `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs:38-41` constructed the
main chunk's `LuaLClosure` with `upvals: Vec::new()`, but `lua_parse::parse` adds the `_ENV
` upvalue to `proto.upvalues` during `mainfunc` (lparser.c
equivalent), so `proto.upvalues.len() == 1`. The assertion in `do_.rs:1613` (`cl.upvals
.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len()`) then failed because 0 ≠ 1. In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` allocates the closure with
`luaF_newLclosure(L, 1)` *before* compil
ation, so the slot count is pre-set; our Rust split returns only the proto from `parse`, le

aving the closure construction to the embedder.

**Fix:** `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs:25-46` — pre-allocate `up
vals` with `proto.upvalues.len()` entries of `UpVal::Closed(LuaValue::Nil)` (matching what `init_upvals` is

meant to populate, which is currently a no-op).

**New error:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1382` — `index out of b
ounds: the len is 0 but the index is 0`. Step 4/4 Executing chunk now reaches the VM and hits the next blocker.

The fix worked — the original assertion at `do_.rs:1613` is gone. Step 3/4 now passes and step 4/4 (Executing chunk) hits a new error at `state.rs:1382` (index out of bounds, len 0 / index 0). That's a different bug — the next blocker for the loop.

## Report

**Root cause:** `parser_hook` in `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs:38-41` constructed the main chunk's `LuaLClosure` with `upvals: Vec::new()`, but `lua_parse::parse` adds the `_ENV` upvalue to `proto.upvalues` during `mainfunc` (lparser.c equivalent), so `proto.upvalues.len() == 1`. The assertion in `do_.rs:1613` (`cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len()`) then failed because 0 ≠ 1. In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` allocates the closure with `luaF_newLclosure(L, 1)` *before* compilation, so the slot count is pre-set; our Rust split returns only the proto from `parse`, leaving the closure construction to the embedder.

**Fix:** `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs:25-46` — pre-allocate `upvals` with `proto.upvalues.len()` entries of `UpVal::Closed(LuaValue::Nil)` (matching what `init_upvals` is meant to populate, which is currently a no-op).

**New error:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1382` — `index out of bounds: the len is 0 but the index is 0`. Step 4/4 Executing chunk now reaches the VM and hits the next blocker.

result:
The fix worked — the original assertion at `do_.rs:1613` is gone. Step 3/4 now passes and step 4/4 (Executing chunk) hits a new error at `state.rs:1382` (index out of bounds, len 0 / index 0). That's a different bug — the next blocker for the loop.

## Report

**Root cause:** `parser_hook` in `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs:38-41` constructed the main chunk's `LuaLClosure` with `upvals: Vec::new()`, but `lua_parse::parse` adds the `_ENV` upvalue to `proto.upvalues` during `mainfunc` (lparser.c equivalent), so `proto.upvalues.len() == 1`. The assertion in `do_.rs:1613` (`cl.upvals.len() == cl.proto.upvalues.len()`) then failed because 0 ≠ 1. In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` allocates the closure with `luaF_newLclosure(L, 1)` *before* compilation, so the slot count is pre-set; our Rust split returns only the proto from `parse`, leaving the closure construction to the embedder.

**Fix:** `crates/lua-cli/src/main.rs:25-46` — pre-allocate `upvals` with `proto.upvalues.len()` entries of `UpVal::Closed(LuaValue::Nil)` (matching what `init_upvals` is meant to populate, which is currently a no-op).

**New error:** `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1382` — `index out of bounds: the len is 0 but the index is 0`. Step 4/4 Executing chunk now reaches the VM and hits the next blocker.
